title: Infernal Flora
description: Adds new variants from minecraft dungeons and emissives to nether flora.
body: _The infernal flora of the nether is alien compared to the overworld: Bioluminescent thermotrophic fungi is the only life that thrives in the dark, barren hellscape of the nether; and the strange warped flora sometimes seem to defy gravity._ This pack seeks to further differentiate the two lush biomes of the nether from eachother, and emphasize what makes them unique compared to the overworld. This is done by adding: hanging plants from the wart blocks, emissives to most flora, and populating the forest floor with more variants. Both optifine and vanilla emissives are used in order to better control the light level. ___ ## Crimson Forest _The crimson forest is a lush habitat with natural, dim bioluminescence. The iron rich ground gives the tree-like fungi, roots and fungi-flowers of the forest floor a vibrant red color. The life here seems to pulsate with a glowing light._ - Emissives to wart blocks - Vines under wart blocks - 5 new root variants, 4 of which are more wart like - 3 new flower variants - 3D mushroom with emissives - Emissives to the stem pattern ![crimson](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/n3bXR0IM/images/d102c7a7dc6ec7e506486322a948b7ef25d55dd8.png) ___ ## Warped Forest _Something seems to be warping and inverting the forest, causing cancerous growth and bright bioluminescence._ - Emissives to wart blocks - 3 new wart block variants - warped moss under wart blocks, 4 variants - New tree mushrooms - 5 new root variants - 1 new flower variants - 3D mushroom with emissives, 4 variants - Emissives to the stem pattern ![pic](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/n3bXR0IM/images/b0063f0f2e078164447992990cd19ae5f405d366.png) ___ **_REQUIRES at LEAST 26.1.x_** Most textures are from Minecraft Dungeons. Feel free to use these assets in your own packs. _**LINKS:**_ ___ [](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/legendary-portals)[](https://modrinth.com/project/improved-portals) [](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/scrofae)
